steps.title: Set up the right triangle from apex to base
steps.content: Let $O$ be the center of the cone's base. Since $AB$ connects apex $A$ to point $B$ on the circumference, and the cone's height is perpendicular from $A$ to $O$, triangle $AOB$ is a right triangle with the right angle at $O$. We have $AB = 78$ cm (slant height), $AO = 39$ cm (height), and $OB = r$ (radius, unknown).

steps.title: Find the radius using the Pythagorean theorem
Apply the cone volume formula to find k
steps.content:
The volume of a cone is $V = \frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$. Substituting $r^2 = 4563$ and $h = 39$:
$$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi (4563)(39)$$
Compute the product: $4563 \times 39 = 177{,}957$.
$$V = \frac{177{,}957}{3}\pi = 59{,}319\pi$$
Therefore $k = 59{,}319$.
